concepts.name: Slippage
concepts.definition: The difference between expected fill price and actual fill price at execution time.
concepts.trader_meaning: How much worse (or better) the trade gets filled versus the quote you saw when deciding.
concepts.why_it_matters: Affects realized PnL, risk, and whether a strategy is viable on a given venue or size.
concepts.decision_impact: Influences sizing, venue choice, and whether to enter a trade at all.
concepts.execution_impact: May warrant limit orders, TWAP, or smaller clips to control adverse selection.
failure_modes: Ignoring slippage when backtesting on mid prices only
Assuming static liquidity during volatile periods
examples: Quoted 100.00, filled at 100.04 on a market buy — 4 bps slippage.
